meta.northy_message: Quiet Saturday. One piece of Garry Tan intel for Monday morning: his keynote on institutional memory as the AI moat. Also — cockpit still uses three colors you retired yesterday. That's the one thing that needs your call.

act_now.action: Call him Monday. Use STATE. Be 100% honest AND 100% respectful.

intel.title: Institutional Memory, Not Model Access, Is the AI-Native Moat
intel.pattern: The durable asset isn't model access (rented). It's accumulated skills, memory, processes, and ability to select relevant context. 'Skill file = employee. Resolver table = org chart.' Never do one-off work — skillify it. GBrain at 220K+ pages, 66 cron jobs.
intel.applies_to: Direct validation of Steve's agent fleet approach. The founding trio (Steve+Northy+Forge) maps perfectly to Garry's 'workforce made of markdown.' Every Bookmarked agent skill is a moat-building asset.

intel.title: gstack supports Hermes natively — /pair-agent names Hermes explicitly
intel.pattern: 23-specialist virtual team. Cross-model review via /codex. /browse and /qa using Playwright CLI instead of Chrome MCP. 121K GitHub stars.
intel.applies_to: Forge evaluate gstack install on Hermes. Focus on /browse, /qa, /autoplan tools.

intel.title: Skill self-modification policy: agents may self-patch, drift caught by review
intel.pattern: Steve reversed morning prohibition within hours — self-learning stays on. Policy settled: agents may self-patch own skills; drift caught in review.
